perturbation.original_text: This Mutual Non -Disclosure Agreement (“Agreement”) is entered into by and between AAGEX Freight Group , LLC (“ AGX ”), and the party identified as agent candidate below (“Candidate”) . AGX and CANDIDATE may be referred to herein individually as a “Party” and jointly as the “Parties.”
perturbation.changed_text: This Mutual Non -Disclosure Agreement (“Agreement”) is entered into by and between AAGEX Freight Group , LLC (“ AGX ”), and the party identified as agent candidate below (“Affiliate”) . AGX and CANDIDATE may be referred to herein individually as a “Party” and jointly as the “Parties.”
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'Candidate' with 'Affiliate', the agreement introduces ambiguity. Throughout the agreement, 'Candidate' is used in the context of a prospective agent. Changing this to 'Affiliate' implies a pre-existing business relationship, which contradicts the background stated in Section 1 that discusses an 'Agency Relationship' that's yet to be established. This contradicts where it says that 'AGX and CANDIDATE desire to exchange information and to engage in discussions concerning AGX’s agent program and Candidate’s qualifications to establish a freight Agency Relationship.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: AGX and CANDIDATE desire to exchange information and to engage in discussions concerning AGX’s agent program and Candidate’s qualifications to establish a freight Agency Relationship. In the course of such discussions and negotiations, it is anticipated that a Party (“Disclosing Party”) may disclose or otherwise make available to the other Party (“Receiving Party”) certain confidential and proprietary inform ation for the purpose of enabling the Receiving Party to evaluate and the prospective business relationship (the “NDA Purpose”). The Parties have entered into this Agreement in order to ensure the confidentiality and proper protection of such confidential and proprietary information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Candidate' to 'Affiliate' introduces ambiguity regarding the nature of the relationship between AGX and the other party. The original text and contradicted text clearly establish that 'Candidate' is someone seeking to establish an 'Agency Relationship'. Changing it to 'Affiliate' implies a pre-existing relationship, directly contradicting the contradicted text where it states that AGX and CANDIDATE 'desire to exchange information and to engage in discussions concerning AGX’s agent program and Candidate’s qualifications to establish a freight Agency Relationship'. This misalignment in terminology leads to a direct conflict regarding the intended meaning and scope of the agreement, fitting the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: In the course of such discussions and negotiations, it is anticipated that a Party (“Disclosing Party”) may disclose or otherwise make available to the other Party (“Receiving Party”) certain confidential and proprietary inform ation for the purpose of enabling the Receiving Party to evaluate and the prospective business relationship (the “NDA Purpose”).
perturbation.changed_text: In the course of such discussions and negotiations, it is anticipated that a Party (“Disclosing Party”) may disclose or otherwise make available to the other Party (“Evaluating Party”) certain confidential and proprietary inform ation for the purpose of enabling the Receiving Party to evaluate and the prospective business relationship (the “NDA Purpose”).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Receiving Party' to 'Evaluating Party' introduces contradiction. 'Receiving Party' is consistently used throughout the agreement, but this section changes it to 'Evaluating Party' with no definition of the difference, potentially creating confusion, particularly in Section 4, which outlines obligations of the Receiving Party. Which one is it? This contradicts the definition that should be applied to Section 4 where it says 'Disclosure and Protection of Confidential Information. The Receiving Party shall (i) hold all Confidential Information in confidence, and shall not disclose Confidential Information except as expressly permitted under the terms of this Agreement and (ii) use Confidential Information only for the NDA Purpose and for no other purpose without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure and Protection of Confidential Information. The Receiving Party shall (i) hold all Confidential Information in confidence, and shall not disclose Confidential Information except as expressly permitted under the terms of this Agreement and (ii) use Confidential Information only for the NDA Purpose and for no other purpose without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Receiving Party' to 'Evaluating Party' in the Changed Text introduces a contradiction with the Contradicted Text. The Contradicted Text explicitly outlines obligations for the 'Receiving Party,' and by changing the term in the Changed Text without defining the new term, it creates ambiguity about which party is bound by the obligations in the Contradicted Text. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, which states that the interchangeable use of terms without definition leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, the term “Confidential Information” shall mean all information that is not generally known or readily ascertainable by proper means by other persons and is either (i) des ignated as “confidential” or “proprietary” by the Disclosing Party prior to or at the time of disclosure or (ii) information that a reasonable person should recognize as being confidential or proprietary, based on the nature of the information and/or the c ircumstances of its disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, the term “Confidential Information” shall mean only written information that is not generally known or readily ascertainable by proper means by other persons and is either (i) des ignated as “confidential” or “proprietary” by the Disclosing Party prior to or at the time of disclosure or (ii) information that a reasonable person should recognize as being confidential or proprietary, based on the nature of the information and/or the c ircumstances of its disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting “Confidential Information” to only 'written' information, the agreement introduces ambiguity. It contradicts with the fact that the information might be disclosed in other formats, such as during the discussion of a new 'Agency Relationship' - Background. It would be ambigous that now, the agreement only covers written documents. This definition contradicts in the first place, where it refers to the 'Background', it mentions that 'AGX and CANDIDATE desire to exchange information and to engage in discussions concerning AGX’s agent program and Candidate’s qualifications to establish a freight Agency Relationship.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Background. AGX and CANDIDATE desire to exchange information and to engage in discussions concerning AGX’s agent program and Candidate’s qualifications to establish a freight Agency Relationship. In the course of such discussions and negotiations, it is anticipated that a Party (“Disclosing Party”) may disclose or otherwise make available to the other Party (“Receiving Party”) certain confidential and proprietary inform ation for the purpose of enabling the Receiving Party to evaluate and the prospective business relationship (the “NDA Purpose”). The Parties have entered into this Agreement in order to ensure the confidentiality and proper protection of such confidential and proprietary information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of "Confidential Information" to only 'written' information. The contradicted text states that AGX and CANDIDATE will 'exchange information' and engage in 'discussions' concerning AGX's agent program. Limiting the definition to 'written' information contradicts the Background section's anticipation of information exchange through discussions, creating a strong in-text contradiction since spoken information would seemingly not be covered by the confidentiality agreement anymore.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information may be either the property of the Disclosing Party or information provided to the Disclosing Party by a third party.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information is always the property of the Disclosing Party, even if it was provided to the Disclosing Party by a third party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information may be either...' to 'Confidential Information is always...' creates a contradiction. The original allows for Confidential Information to originate from a third party. This change implies that regardless of origin, ownership vests solely with the Disclosing Party, which may conflict with the third party's rights or agreements. It also means that the third party would have no rights regarding that Confidential Information, contradicting what would be expected in an NDA. This contradicts what is being discussed regarding the purpose of the NDA to protect third party information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the course of such discussions and negotiations, it is anticipated that a Party (“Disclosing Party”) may disclose or otherwise make available to the other Party (“Receiving Party”) certain confidential and proprietary inform ation for the purpose of enabling the Receiving Party to evaluate and the prospective business relationship (the “NDA Purpose”).
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information may be either...' to 'Confidential Information is always...' directly contradicts the purpose of the NDA as stated in the Contradicted Text: '...a Party (“Disclosing Party”) may disclose or otherwise make available to the other Party (“Receiving Party”) certain confidential and proprietary information for the purpose of enabling the Receiving Party to evaluate and the prospective business relationship (the “NDA Purpose”).' The original text allows the Disclosing Party to disclose information from a third party, implying that the third party retains some rights. However, the Changed Text asserts that all Confidential Information is always the property of the Disclosing Party, regardless of its origin. This contradicts the NDA Purpose, as it could prevent the Receiving Party from using or protecting the third party's confidential information as intended. The third party's information would no longer be protected since the disclosing party now owns all rights according to the changed text. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because the definition of "Confidential Information" is effectively altered in a way that conflicts with the underlying purpose and expectations established in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of the Receiving Party with respect to the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information shall not apply with respect to any Confidential Information to the extent the Receiving Party can demonstrate that such Confidential Information: (a) is generally known to the public at the time of disclosure or becomes generally known through no breach of this Agreement; (b) is in the Receiving Party’s rightful possession at the time of disclosure without an obligation to hold it in Page 2 of 2 confidence; (c) becomes known to the Receiving Party without obligation of confidence through disclosure by a third party h aving the legal right to disclose such information; (d) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without access or reference to, or reliance upon, the Confidential Information ; or (e) is or becomes disclosed through the ordinary course of the NDA Purpose or as an element of any marketing plan intended to be released in the public domain or to targeted geographic public domain .
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of the Receiving Party with respect to the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information shall not apply with respect to any Confidential Information to the extent the Receiving Party can demonstrate that such Confidential Information: (a) is generally known to the public at the time of disclosure or becomes generally known through no fault of this Agreement; (b) is in the Receiving Party’s rightful possession at the time of disclosure with an obligation to hold it in Page 2 of 2 confidence; (c) becomes known to the Receiving Party with obligation of confidence through disclosure by a third party h aving the legal right to disclose such information; (d) is independently developed by the Receiving Party with access or reference to, or reliance upon, the Confidential Information ; or (e) is or becomes disclosed through the ordinary course of the NDA Purpose or as an element of any marketing plan intended to be not released in the public domain or to targeted geographic public domain .
perturbation.explanation: Changing the clauses to their inverse in Section 6 creates direct contradictions to the rest of the document. Making something that was supposed to NOT happen, to happen. The purpose of the agreement is to protect confidentiality. By inverting all of the clauses it negates the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties have entered into this Agreement in order to ensure the confidentiality and proper protection of such confidential and proprietary information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly inverts several key conditions related to confidentiality. Specifically, clauses that previously allowed information to be exempt from confidentiality if it was already public, rightfully possessed without obligation, or independently developed WITHOUT reference to confidential information are changed to impose confidentiality obligations in similar circumstances. This directly contradicts the stated purpose of the agreement, which is to ensure the confidentiality of information, and the fact that parties are trying to protect their confidential information as stated in the 'Contradicted Text.' The explanation states this creates a direct contradiction, which aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, which says this occurs when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall, upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, return to the Disclos ing Party or, at the Disclosing Party’s direction, destroy all documents and any and all other tangible manifestations of Confidential Information received or created by the Receiving Party pursuant to this Agreement (and all copies and reproductions there of).
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall, upon the written request of a third party, return to the Disclos ing Party or, at the Disclosing Party’s direction, destroy all documents and any and all other tangible manifestations of Confidential Information received or created by the Receiving Party pursuant to this Agreement (and all copies and reproductions there of).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Disclosing Party' to 'third party' in the return of documents clause creates a potential conflict. The agreement is between AGX and the Candidate, not a third party. Giving a third party the right to demand the return of confidential information undermines the Disclosing Party's control and creates uncertainty. This contradicts the initial agreement which states 'This Mutual Non -Disclosure Agreement (“Agreement”) is entered into by and between AAGEX Freight Group , LLC (“ AGX ”), and the party identified as agent candidate below (“Candidate”) .'
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Mutual Non -Disclosure Agreement (“Agreement”) is entered into by and between AAGEX Freight Group , LLC (“ AGX ”), and the party identified as agent candidate below (“Candidate”) . AGX and CANDIDATE may be referred to herein individually as a “Party” and jointly as the “Parties.”
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by replacing "Disclosing Party" with "third party." This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text specifies that the agreement is between AGX and the Candidate. Allowing a third party to request the return of confidential information directly contradicts the core agreement outlined in the contradicted text, creating uncertainty and undermining the Disclosing Party's control over confidential information. This creates a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, according to the definition.
